title: invitations — cross-module relations
description: Eloquent relations rooted at this module's models. Target + inviter are polymorphic — this module never name-drops a concrete consumer. Every downstream module registers its `target_type` string via the InvitationTargetRegistry container binding at boot; the morph resolver reads the registry to hydrate the concrete model. Cross-tenant FKs remain forbidden — tenant_id is nullable ONLY to accommodate platform-scope invitations (Stackra staff onboarding).

relations.name: tenant
relations.description: NULL for platform-scope invitations. BelongsToTenantOptional trait skips the global scope when tenant_id is null.

relations.name: acceptedBy
relations.description: The User row that resulted from the accept flow. NULL until state=accepted.

relations.name: revokedBy
relations.description: The User who revoked. NULL until state=revoked.

cascade.description: Hard-delete cascades to events. Retention purge respects it.

morphs.name: target
morphs.description: The invitable resource — Tenant, Team, Athlete, Federation, TrialSession, ... Concrete class resolved by InvitationTargetRegistry at hydrate time.

morphs.name: inviter
morphs.description: The actor that sent the invitation. Registered inviter kinds: `user`, `service_account`, `system` (NULL inviter_id for system).

expectedInviters.note: Platform-initiated invitations (Stackra ops, automated flows). inviter_id is NULL.

morphs.name: actor
morphs.description: Who triggered this event. NULL for transport-reported events (`delivered`, `bounced`) where the acting party is an external system. Registered actor kinds: `user`, `service_account`, `system`, `invitee` (email recipient), `mail_transport`, `webhook`.

notes: Polymorphic target/inviter/actor are the extension seams. The invitations module does NOT know that `tenant` means Tenancy's Tenant — it just knows a string key + registration hooked at boot.

InvitationTargetRegistry is a container binding published by this module. Downstream modules register their target_type + accept handler at boot. Adding a new invitable resource = one class + one register() call. Zero schema change.
Tenant + Application are hard FKs — polymorphism ends at the tenancy boundary; the tenancy substrate is universal.
